title: Kids Deserve Better: World Day Against Child Labor
story: Every June 12, people around the world unite for World Day Against Child Labor to make sure all kids get to be kids. Schools organize special assemblies where students learn about children's rights. Communities hold walks and rallies with colorful banners that say "Education, Not Work!" Families talk about how every child deserves time to play, learn, and dream instead of working dangerous jobs. Social media fills with photos of kids playing and studying, showing what childhood should look like everywhere.
activity_question: What do communities often hold on World Day Against Child Labor?
follow_up_question: What would you want to tell kids around the world about their right to play and learn?
